_notes: Coaching-rule registry (v7.67). Maps a rule/leak code to its coaching
source and a concise plain-language meaning so reports can render codes
as 'Source - meaning' instead of an opaque code.
Source convention (an invariant enforced by gem_coaching.rule_source):
J* = Dave, N* = Amit, K* = Jaka. L* (leak codes) carry an explicit tier.
Extend by adding an entry here - no code change needed. Only add a code
once its definition is verified; gem_coaching.describe() falls back to the
bare code for anything unregistered, so omissions never regress output.
Seeded with the verified Dave/Amit/Jaka rule + leak set; M/HH/MDA/V codes
are intentionally not yet included (different sources, pending verification).

J14.label: Monotone flop: c-bet only when you hold none of that suit

J29.label: SB blind-vs-blind entry is limp-heavy - roughly 80% limp / 10% raise / 10% fold

J34.label: ICM short-stack MP: raise or 3-bet AQo/AJs/ATs/AJo - never flat-call

J35.label: At <=30BB facing a small jam: call or fold - no profitable reshove range exists

J36.label: ICM open-jams at 30-37BB can be oversized - a normal 3-bet captures the same EV

J43.label: In ICM phases, raise or fold from out-of-position seats - don't flat-call

J44.label: IP 3-bet sizing scales with depth - larger 3-bets as stacks get deeper

N1.label: Weak Ax (A2-A8) facing a 3-bet or squeeze: fold or shove - never flat

N3.label: Don't fold JTs to a BB jam at <=30BB in blind-vs-blind

N8.label: At <=8BB from CO/BTN, open-shove offsuit Kx (K2o-K9o) rather than folding

N9.label: MP facing a UTG/UTG+1 open: don't flat-call ATo at >=25BB - 3-bet or fold

N12.label: KTs in MP vs a UTG open: 3-bet, don't flat-call

N13.label: SB with a pair vs a late-position open at <=30BB: shove - don't 3-bet then fold

K1.label: Raising c-bets in position: value is overpair+ and top-pair-best-kicker; bluff flush draws over straight draws

K2.label: Out-of-position as PFR, default to checking - keep a genuine checking range

K3.label: As the in-position caller, stab 40-60% when the PFR checks - stable across textures

K4.label: Single-raised-pot c-bet frequency vs the SB scales with opener position and stack depth

K5.label: Range advantage -> bet wide and small; only-nut advantage -> narrow and big; monotone -> narrow and small

K6.label: Flop leading (donking) is rare - about 2.5% baseline, reserved for specific spots

L1.label: Stop barreling a check-raised flop once your equity dies - bet-or-fold the turn

L2.label: Don't jam to skip postflop play - if a small bet won't get value, a jam isn't value either

L3.label: In multiway 3-bet pots, read villains' prior action before jamming - a cold flat caps their range

L4.label: Don't raise a dominated second-best hand into a narrow polarized range - call or fold instead

L5.label: On low dry boards in position as PFR, take the range-bet - don't under-c-bet

N14.label: SB facing an open (incl. EP) at ~30BB+ eff: 3-bet AXs and KQo rather than flat-call - flatting builds a capped, dominated SB range

N15.label: Facing a UTG open from outside CO/BTN: 3-bet or fold weak Ax (kicker <8, A2s-A7s) - don't cold-flat into a dominated spot / dead money

N16.label: Choose bet size by the bottom of villain's calling range - that fraction is what you keep. Oversizing value denies the calls that pay you; especially OOP, size to the hands you want to call

N17.label: IP c-betting air on dynamic boards: use one small size, not the GTO big/dual sizing - the pool calls 2BB and 3BB with the same hands, so the big size only loses more when called

N18.label: vs good regs in capped lines (check/check flop and turn): bet big on the river for thin value - they call with bluff-catchers
